title: デジタル庁デザインシステム (DADS)
summary: Japan Digital Agency's design system (DADS) β版, observed at v2.16.0 on 2026-07-16. Covers Japanese design language, UI components, usability/accessibility implementation guidelines for administrative digital services.
lessons: Create a ja-public-service evidence profile: Japanese typography, form/error clarity, administrative-task resilience, and keyboard/AT behavior.
Compare directly with GOV.UK and USWDS for cross-government design system benchmarking.
caveats: DADS is labeled β版; version-specific claims must cite the observed version (v2.16.0).
Guidelines are scoped to Japanese government digital services; adapt usability patterns without importing administrative branding.
licenseNote: Public government documentation; verify component code license terms separately.
antiImitationNote: Transfer Japanese public-service usability patterns and accessibility guidance; do not import government branding, logos, or administrative-specific visual identity.
